commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Codex app-server gate that disables the native tool surface when the effective agent or global `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` policy is true, plus a mixed-agent unit test.

PR surface: Source +87, Tests +37. Total +124 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main enables the broad Codex native surface when sandbox and allowlist gates pass, while OpenClaw-owned file tools separately enforce `tools.fs.workspaceOnly`. I did not establish a live after-fix Codex run for this PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the Codex security and compatibility strategy, and the contributor needs real behavior proof before this PR can move toward merge.

target.title: fix(codex): honor workspace-only fs for native tools
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
When an OpenClaw agent sets `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`, OpenClaw-owned file tools enforce that boundary. The Codex app-server native tool surface can bypass that policy if left enabled, because native Codex tools are not constrained by OpenClaw's workspace-only fs wrapper. This disables the native tool surface for the effective workspace-only fs policy while leaving non-workspace-only agents unchanged.

## Summary
- disable Codex app-server native tool surfaces when effective OpenClaw fs policy is workspace-only
- resolve the policy from the active agent, runtime/session key, default agent, then global config
- cover mixed-agent config where one agent is workspace-only and another is not

## Evidence
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts` passed locally: 1 file, 50 tests
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Notes
Full repo extension typecheck currently fails on unrelated `acpx` and `qa-lab` / `@openclaw/crabline` errors before this patch-specific surface can be isolated.
